page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n1 server family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running on an Intel Xeon CPU at 2.3 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, and the system is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.75 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The CPU supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets, but lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the n1-standard-8 occupies a lower-to-mid-tier performance profile. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng place the instance in the bottom 25%. Similarly, single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, register in the bottom 10%. However, the instance achieves average tier results in large-block memory bandwidth read and write tests, as well as in Geekbench ray tracing and Passmark CPU and memory scores. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput remain in the bottom 25%, reflecting limitations in high-throughput database and web serving workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-standard-8 offers a balanced allocation of dedicated CPU and memory resources for legacy or non-demanding workloads without the premium cost of newer hardware generations. Given the weak single-core performance and lower memory bandwidth, it is best suited for general-purpose tasks, moderate development environments, basic background processing, or low-intensity text generation using small language models. It is less suitable for high-performance computing, intensive database operations, or large-scale web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 30.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 4 physical cores. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance primarily in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks scoring in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. However, it maintains average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and Passmark CPU tests. Lacking AVX-512 and local storage, this instance represents a legacy hardware profile. It is qualitatively cost-effective for steady-state, low-intensity workloads such as basic development environments, small-scale text generation, and general-purpose utility tasks, rather than high-throughput database operations or intensive web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, suited for moderate utility workloads and basic development environments.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-8 is a general-purpose VM with 8 vCPUs and 30 GB RAM, powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon resources for steady-state, moderate-intensity general-purpose workloads.
